mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive quarterly returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces rate hikes, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration coincides with negative weekly returns in >65% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of periods following positive GDP growth revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly returns remain positive in >60% of instances when inflation exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The employment-unemployment divergence fails to predict positive monthly returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a liquidity-constrained regime signal weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly returns are positive in >60% of periods following housing start declines during liquidity constraints.
